content.text: I'll implement this MTL (Mini Transformation Language) interpreter. Let me start by creating a plan and setting up the environment.

todos.activeForm: Setting up virtual environment and project structure
todos.content: Set up virtual environment and project structure

todos.activeForm: Implementing CSV reader and data structures
todos.content: Implement CSV reader and data structures
Build DSL parser for all statement types
Implement type system and validation
Implement filter operations with boolean expressions
Implement apply transforms (len, day, month, year, round)
Implement windowing (tumbling windows)
Implement group_by functionality
Implement aggregations (sum, avg, median, count, std, var, min, max)
Implement JSON output formatting with normalization rules
Add error handling and CLI interface
Test with sample data and verify determinism

750→    def check_transform(self, transform: TransformExpr) -> str:
   751→        """Check transform expression and return its output type."""
   752→        if isinstance(transform, LenTransform):
   753→            col_type = self.get_col_type(transform.col)
   754→            if col_type != DataType.STRING:
   755→                raise TypeError(f"len() requires string column, got '{col_type}' for '{transform.col}'")
   756→            return DataType.NUMERIC
   757→
   758→        elif isinstance(transform, DateTransform):
   759→            col_type = self.get_col_type(transform.col)
   760→            if col_type != DataType.TIMESTAMP:
   761→                raise TypeError(f"{transform.func}() requires timestamp column, got '{col_type}' for '{transform.col}'")
   762→            return DataType.NUMERIC
   763→
   764→        elif isinstance(transform, RoundTransform):
   765→            col_type = self.get_col_type(transform.col)
   766→            if col_type != DataType.NUMERIC:
   767→                raise TypeError(f"round() requires numeric column, got '{col_type}' for '{transform.col}'")
   768→            if transform.digits is not None and not isinstance(transform.digits, int):
   769→                raise TypeError(f"round() digits must be an integer")
   770→            return DataType.NUMERIC
   771→
   772→        raise TypeError(f"Unknown transform type")
   773→
   774→    def check_bool_expr(self, expr: BoolExpr):
   775→        """Check boolean expression for type correctness."""
   776→        if isinstance(expr, BinOpBool):
   777→            self.check_bool_expr(expr.left)
   778→            self.check_bool_expr(expr.right)
   779→
   780→        elif isinstance(expr, NotBool):
   781→            self.check_bool_expr(expr.expr)
   782→
   783→        elif isinstance(expr, CompBool):
   784→            # Check types are compatible
   785→            left_type = self._eval_scalar_type(expr.left)
   786→            right_type = self._eval_scalar_type(expr.right)
   787→
   788→            # Numeric allows int/float mixing
   789→            if left_type == DataType.NUMERIC and right_type == DataType.NUMERIC:
   790→                return
   791→            if left_type == right_type:
   792→                return
   793→            raise TypeError(f"Incompatible types for comparison: '{left_type}' and '{right_type}'")
   794→
   795→        elif isinstance(expr, InBool):
   796→            # left must be string literal, right must be string column
   797→            # Type checker already validated the literal
   798→            col_type = self.get_col_type(expr.col)
   799→            if col_type != DataType.STRING:
   800→                raise TypeError(f"'in' operator requires string column, got '{col_type}' for '{expr.col}'")
   801→
   802→        elif isinstance(expr, ParenBool):
   803→            self.check_bool_expr(expr.expr)
   804→
   805→        elif isinstance(expr, BoolLiteral):
   806→            pass  # Always valid
   807→
   808→    def _eval_scalar_type(self, expr: ScalarExpr) -> str:
   809→        """Evaluate the type of a scalar expression."""
   810→        if isinstance(expr, ColumnRef):
   811→            return self.get_col_type(expr.name)
   812→        elif isinstance(expr, StringLiteral):
   813→            return DataType.STRING
   814→        elif isinstance(expr, NumberLiteral):
   815→            return DataType.NUMERIC
   816→        elif isinstance(expr, BoolLiteral):
   817→            return DataType.BOOLEAN
   818→        raise TypeError(f"Unknown scalar expression type")
   819→
   820→    def check_aggregation(self, agg: Aggregation) -> str:
   821→        """Check aggregation and return its output type."""
   822→        valid_fns = {'average', 'median', 'sum', 'count', 'std', 'var', 'min', 'max'}
   823→
   824→        if agg.fn not in valid_fns:
   825→            raise TypeError(f"Unknown aggregation function '{agg.fn}'")
   826→
   827→        if agg.fn == 'count':
   828→            if agg.col is None:
   829→                return DataType.NUMERIC  # count(*) always numeric
   830→            # count(column) is also numeric
   831→            col_type = self.get_col_type(agg.col)
   832→            if col_type not in (DataType.NUMERIC, DataType.STRING, DataType.TIMESTAMP, DataType.BOOLEAN):
   833→                raise TypeError(f"count() column must have a valid type, got '{col_type}'")
   834→            return DataType.NUMERIC
   835→
   836→        # All other aggregations require numeric input
   837→        if agg.col is None:
   838→            raise TypeError(f"{agg.fn}() requires a column argument")
   839→
   840→        col_type = self.get_col_type(agg.col)
   841→        if col_type != DataType.NUMERIC:
   842→            raise TypeError(f"{agg.fn}() requires numeric column, got '{col_type}'")
   843→
   844→        return DataType.NUMERIC
   845→
   846→
   847→# =============================================================================
   848→# Data Processor
   849→# =============================================================================
   850→
   851→class DataProcessor:
   852→    """Executes the transformation pipeline on data."""
   853→
   854→    def __init__(self, data: pd.DataFrame, tz: str = 'UTC'):
   855→        self.data = data.copy()
   856→        self.tz = tz
   857→        self.epoch = datetime(1970, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   858→
   859→        # Infer types from data
   860→        self.col_types: Dict[str, str] = {}
   861→        for col in data.columns:
   862→            # Try to infer type from first non-null value
   863→            sample = data[col].dropna().iloc[0] if len(data[col].dropna()) > 0 else None
   864→            if sample is not None:
   865→                self.col_types[col] = infer_dtype(sample)
   866→            else:
   867→                # Default to string for all-null columns
   868→                self.col_types[col] = DataType.STRING
   869→
   870→    def get_col_type(self, col: str) -> str:
   871→        if col not in self.col_types:
   872→            raise TypeError(f"Unknown column '{col}'")
   873→        return self.col_types[col]
   874→
   875→    def add_col(self, name: str, values: pd.Series, dtype: str):
   876→        """Add a new column."""
   877→        self.data[name] = values
   878→        self.col_types[name] = dtype
   879→
   880→    def execute_filter(self, stmt: FilterStatement) -> 'DataProcessor':
   881→        """Execute filter statement."""
   882→        mask = self._eval_bool_expr(stmt.expr)
   883→        new_proc = DataProcessor(self.data[mask], self.tz)
   884→        new_proc.col_types = self.col_types.copy()
   885→        return new_proc
   886→
   887→    def _eval_bool_expr(self, expr: BoolExpr) -> pd.Series:
   888→        """Evaluate boolean expression to a boolean mask."""
   889→        if isinstance(expr, BinOpBool):
   890→            left = self._eval_bool_expr(expr.left)
   891→            right = self._eval_bool_expr(expr.right)
   892→            if expr.op == '&':
   893→                return left & right
   894→            else:  # '|'
   895→                return left | right
   896→
   897→        elif isinstance(expr, NotBool):
   898→            return ~self._eval_bool_expr(expr.expr)
   899→
   900→        elif isinstance(expr, CompBool):
   901→            left_vals = self._eval_scalar(expr.left)
   902→            right_vals = self._eval_scalar(expr.right)
   903→
   904→            # Handle numeric comparisons (allow int/float mixing)
   905→            left_type = self._infer_series_type(left_vals)
   906→            right_type = self._infer_series_type(right_vals)
   907→
   908→            if left_type == DataType.NUMERIC and right_type == DataType.NUMERIC:
   909→                # Convert both to float for comparison
   910→                left_f = pd.to_numeric(left_vals, errors='coerce')
   911→                right_f = pd.to_numeric(right_vals, errors='coerce')
   912→
   913→                if expr.op == '<':
   914→                    return left_f < right_f
   915→                elif expr.op == '<=':
   916→                    return left_f <= right_f
   917→                elif expr.op == '>':
   918→                    return left_f > right_f
   919→                elif expr.op == '>=':
   920→                    return left_f >= right_f
   921→                elif expr.op == '==':
   922→                    return left_f == right_f
   923→                elif expr.op == '!=':
   924→                    return left_f != right_f
   925→
   926→            elif left_type == right_type:
   927→                if expr.op == '<':
   928→                    return left_vals < right_vals
   929→                elif expr.op == '<=':
   930→                    return left_vals <= right_vals
   931→                elif expr.op == '>':
   932→                    return left_vals > right_vals
   933→                elif expr.op == '>=':
   934→                    return left_vals >= right_vals
   935→                elif expr.op == '==':
   936→                    return left_vals == right_vals
   937→                elif expr.op == '!=':
   938→                    return left_vals != right_vals
   939→
   940→            raise TypeError(f"Incompatible types for comparison: '{left_type}' and '{right_type}'")
   941→
   942→        elif isinstance(expr, InBool):
   943→            col_series = self.data[expr.col]
   944→            # Check if substring exists in each value
   945→            return col_series.astype(str).str.contains(expr.literal, regex=False, na=False)
   946→
   947→        elif isinstance(expr, ParenBool):
   948→            return self._eval_bool_expr(expr.expr)
   949→

  1409→        # Initialize processor

  1411→
  1412→        # Re-infer types after timestamp parsing

  1414→
  1415→        # Execute pipeline
  1416→        group_cols = []
  1417→        duration = None
  1418→        aggregate_stmt = None
  1419→
  1420→        for stmt in statements:
  1421→            if isinstance(stmt, FilterStatement):
  1422→                # Type check
  1423→                type_checker = TypeChecker(processor.data, processor.col_types)
  1424→                type_checker.check_bool_expr(stmt.expr)
  1425→                processor = processor.execute_filter(stmt)
  1426→
  1427→            elif isinstance(stmt, ApplyStatement):
  1428→                # Type check
  1429→                result_type = processor._check_transform_type(stmt.transform_expr)
  1430→                processor = processor.execute_apply(stmt)
  1431→
  1432→            elif isinstance(stmt, GroupByStatement):
  1433→                group_cols = stmt.columns
  1434→                processor = processor.execute_group_by(stmt)
  1435→
  1436→            elif isinstance(stmt, WindowStatement):

  1440→            elif isinstance(stmt, AggregateStatement):

  1443→        # Execute aggregation
  1444→        results = processor.execute_aggregate(aggregate_stmt)
  1445→
  1446→        # Collect aggregate aliases
  1447→        agg_aliases = [agg.alias for agg in aggregate_stmt.aggregations]
  1448→
  1449→        # Normalize output
  1450→        normalized = normalize_output(results, group_cols, has_window, agg_aliases)
  1451→
  1452→        # Format timestamps in output
  1453→        for result in normalized:
  1454→            if 'window_start' in result:
  1455→                result['window_start'] = format_timestamp(result['window_start'])
  1456→            if 'window_end' in result:
  1457→                result['window_end'] = format_timestamp(result['window_end'])
  1458→
  1459→        # Write output
  1460→        output_json = json.dumps(normalized, indent=2) if len(normalized) > 1 else json.dumps(normalized[0] if normalized else {})
  1461→
  1462→        if args.out:
  1463→            with open(args.out, 'w') as f:
  1464→                f.write(output_json + '\n')
  1465→        else:
  1466→            print(output_json)
  1467→
  1468→        return 0
  1469→
  1470→    except DLSError as e:
  1471→        print(e, file=sys.stderr)
  1472→        return 1
  1473→    except TypeError as e:
  1474→        print(e, file=sys.stderr)
  1475→        return 2
  1476→    except IOError as e:
  1477→        print(e, file=sys.stderr)
  1478→        return 3
  1479→    except RuntimeError as e:
  1480→        print(e, file=sys.stderr)
  1481→        return 4
  1482→    except Exception as e:
  1483→        # General error
  1484→        print(f"ERROR:unknown:{str(e)}", file=sys.stderr)
  1485→        return 1
  1486→
  1487→
  1488→if __name__ == '__main__':
  1489→    sys.exit(main())
  1490→

# Skip to end of line
                while self.pos < self.length and self.text[self.pos] != '\n':
                    self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Handle empty lines (we'll handle them later in parser)
            if char == '\n':
                self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Check for comparison operators (multi-char first to check ==, !=, etc.)
            for op in self.COMPARISON_OPERATORS:
                if self.text.startswith(op, self.pos):
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.COMP, op, self.pos))
                    self.pos += len(op)
                    break
            else:
                # Check for single char operators
                if char in self.SINGLEChar_OPERATORS:
                    self.tokens.append(Token(self.SINGLEChar_OPERATORS[char], char, self.pos))
                    self.pos += 1
                    continue

                # Check for IN keyword
                if self.text.startswith('in', self.pos):
                    # Make sure it's not part of an identifier
                    next_pos = self.pos + 2
                    if next_pos >= self.length or not self.text[next_pos].isalnum():
                        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IN, 'in', self.pos))
                        self.pos += 2
                        continue

                # Handle strings
                if char == "'":
                    start = self.pos
                    self.pos += 1
                    string_value = ''
                    while self.pos < self.length and self.text[self.pos] != "'":
                        string_value += self.text[self.pos]
                        self.pos += 1
                    if self.pos >= self.length:
                        raise DLSError(f"Unterminated string starting at position {start}")
                    self.pos += 1  # Skip closing quote
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.STRING, string_value, start))
                    continue

                # Handle numbers
                if char.isdigit() or (char == '.' and self.pos + 1 < self.length and self.text[self.pos + 1].isdigit()):
                    start = self.pos
                    has_decimal = (char == '.')
                    self.pos += 1
                    while self.pos < self.length:
                        c = self.text[self.pos]
                        if c.isdigit() or (c == '.' and not has_decimal):
                            if c == '.':
                                has_decimal = True
                            self.pos += 1
                        else:
                            break
                    value = self.text[start:self.pos]
                    token_type = TokenType.NUMBER
                    self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, start))
                    continue

                # Handle boolean literals
                if self.text.startswith('true', self.pos) or self.text.startswith('false', self.pos):
                    end = self.pos + 4 if self.text.startswith('true', self.pos) else self.pos + 5
                    if end <= self.length:
                        # Check it's not part of a longer identifier
                        if end == self.length or not self.text[end].isalnum():
                            value = self.text[self.pos:end]
                            self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.BOOL, value, self.pos))
                            self.pos = end
                            continue

                # Handle identifiers (bare words)
                start = self.pos
                while self.pos < self.length and (self.text[self.pos].isalnum() or self.text[self.pos] == '_'):
                    self.pos += 1
                ident = self.text[start:self.pos]
                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, ident, start))
                continue

        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.EOF, '', self.pos))
        return self.tokens


# =============================================================================
# AST Node Classes
# =============================================================================

class AST:
    """Base class for AST nodes."""
    pass


class FilterStatement(AST):
    """filter(<bool_expr>)"""
    def __init__(self, expr):
        self.expr = expr

file.content:
"""Tokenizer for MTL DSL."""

    COMPARISON_OPERATORS = ['==', '!=', '<=', '>=', '<', '>']
    SINGLEChar_OPERATORS = {
        '(': TokenType.LPAREN,
        ')': TokenType.RPAREN,
        ',': TokenType.COMMA,
        '=': TokenType.EQUALS,
        '[': TokenType.LBRACKET,
        ']': TokenType.RBRACKET,
        '.': TokenType.DOT,
        ':': TokenType.COLON,
        '&': TokenType.AND,
        '|': TokenType.OR,
        '!': TokenType.NOT,
    }

    def __init__(self, text: str):
        self.text = text
        self.pos = 0
        self.length = len(text)
        self.tokens: List[Token] = []

    def tokenize(self) -> List[Token]:
        """Convert source text to tokens."""
        while self.pos < self.length:
            char = self.text[self.pos]

            # Skip whitespace
            if char.isspace():
                self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Handle line comments
            if char == '#':
                # Skip to end of line
                while self.pos < self.length and self.text[self.pos] != '\n':
                    self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Handle empty lines (we'll handle them later in parser)
            if char == '\n':
                self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Check for comparison operators (multi-char first to check ==, !=, etc.)
            for op in self.COMPARISON_OPERATORS:
                if self.text.startswith(op, self.pos):
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.COMP, op, self.pos))
                    self.pos += len(op)

   208→                    self.pos += 1

   212→

   214→        return self.tokens
   215→

   217→# =============================================================================
   218→# AST Node Classes
   219→# =============================================================================
   220→
   221→class AST:
   222→    """Base class for AST nodes."""
   223→    pass
   224→
   225→
   226→class FilterStatement(AST):
   227→    """filter(<bool_expr>)"""
   228→    def __init__(self, expr):
   229→        self.expr = expr
   230→
   231→
   232→class ApplyStatement(AST):
   233→    """apply(<transform_expr>, <new_col_name>)"""
   234→    def __init__(self, transform_expr: 'TransformExpr', new_col: str):
   235→        self.transform_expr = transform_expr
   236→        self.new_col = new_col
   237→
   238→
   239→class GroupByStatement(AST):
   240→    """group_by(<col_name> | [<col_name>, ...])"""
   241→    def __init__(self, columns: List[str]):
   242→        self.columns = columns
   243→
   244→
   245→class WindowStatement(AST):
   246→    """window(<duration_literal>)"""
   247→    def __init__(self, duration: str):
   248→        self.duration = duration
   249→
   250→
   251→class AggregateStatement(AST):
   252→    """aggregate <agg_list>"""
   253→    def __init__(self, aggregations: List['Aggregation']):
   254→        self.aggregations = aggregations
   255→
   256→
   257→class Aggregation:
   258→    """<fn>(<col>|*) as <alias>"""
   259→    def __init__(self, fn: str, col: Optional[str], alias: str):
   260→        self.fn = fn
   261→        self.col = col  # None for count(*)
   262→        self.alias = alias
   263→
   264→
   265→class TransformExpr:
   266→    """Base class for transform expressions."""
   267→    pass
   268→
   269→
   270→class LenTransform(TransformExpr):
   271→    """len(<string_col>) -> int"""
   272→    def __init__(self, col: str):
   273→        self.col = col
   274→
   275→
   276→class DateTransform(TransformExpr):
   277→    """day|month|year(<timestamp_col>) -> int"""
   278→    def __init__(self, func: str, col: str):
   279→        self.func = func  # 'day', 'month', or 'year'
   280→        self.col = col
   281→
   282→
   283→class RoundTransform(TransformExpr):
   284→    """round(<number_col> [, <digits:int>]) -> number"""
   285→    def __init__(self, col: str, digits: Optional[int] = None):
   286→        self.col = col
   287→        self.digits = digits
   288→
   289→
   290→# Boolean expression AST nodes
   291→class BoolExpr(AST):
   292→    """Base class for boolean expressions."""
   293→    pass
   294→
   295→
   296→class BinOpBool(BoolExpr):
   297→    def __init__(self, left: BoolExpr, op: str, right: BoolExpr):
   298→        self.left = left
   299→        self.op = op  # '&' or '|'

file.content:
def __init__(self, text: str):
        self.text = text
        self.pos = 0
        self.length = len(text)
        self.tokens: List[Token] = []

    def tokenize(self) -> List[Token]:
        """Convert source text to tokens."""
        while self.pos < self.length:
            char = self.text[self.pos]

            # Skip whitespace
            if char.isspace():
                self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Handle line comments
            if char == '#':
                # Skip to end of line
                while self.pos < self.length and self.text[self.pos] != '\n':
                    self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Handle empty lines (we'll handle them later in parser)
            if char == '\n':
                self.pos += 1
                continue

            # Check for comparison operators (multi-char first to check ==, !=, etc.)
            for op in self.COMPARISON_OPERATORS:
                if self.text.startswith(op, self.pos):
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.COMP, op, self.pos))
                    self.pos += len(op)
                    break
            else:
                # Check for single char operators
                if char in self.SINGLEChar_OPERATORS:
                    self.tokens.append(Token(self.SINGLEChar_OPERATORS[char], char, self.pos))
                    self.pos += 1
                    continue

                # Check for IN keyword
                if self.text.startswith('in', self.pos):
                    # Make sure it's not part of an identifier
                    next_pos = self.pos + 2
                    if next_pos >= self.length or not self.text[next_pos].isalnum():
                        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IN, 'in', self.pos))
                        self.pos += 2
                        continue

                # Handle strings
                if char == "'":
                    start = self.pos
                    self.pos += 1
                    string_value = ''
                    while self.pos < self.length and self.text[self.pos] != "'":
                        string_value += self.text[self.pos]
                        self.pos += 1
                    if self.pos >= self.length:
                        raise DLSError(f"Unterminated string starting at position {start}")
                    self.pos += 1  # Skip closing quote
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.STRING, string_value, start))
                    continue

                # Handle numbers
                if char.isdigit() or (char == '.' and self.pos + 1 < self.length and self.text[self.pos + 1].isdigit()):
                    start = self.pos
                    has_decimal = (char == '.')
                    self.pos += 1
                    while self.pos < self.length:
                        c = self.text[self.pos]
                        if c.isdigit() or (c == '.' and not has_decimal):
                            if c == '.':
                                has_decimal = True
                            self.pos += 1
                        else:
                            break
                    value = self.text[start:self.pos]
                    token_type = TokenType.NUMBER
                    self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, start))
                    continue

                # Handle boolean literals
                if self.text.startswith('true', self.pos) or self.text.startswith('false', self.pos):
                    end = self.pos + 4 if self.text.startswith('true', self.pos) else self.pos + 5
                    if end <= self.length:
                        # Check it's not part of a longer identifier
                        if end == self.length or not self.text[end].isalnum():
                            value = self.text[self.pos:end]
                            self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.BOOL, value, self.pos))
                            self.pos = end
                            continue

                # Handle identifiers (bare words)
                start = self.pos
                while self.pos < self.length and (self.text[self.pos].isalnum() or self.text[self.pos] == '_'):
                    self.pos += 1
                ident = self.text[start:self.pos]
                self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, ident, start))
                continue

input.old_string:
# Check for single char operators
                if char in self.SINGLEChar_OPERATORS:
                    self.tokens.append(Token(self.SINGLEChar_OPERATORS[char], char, self.pos))
                    self.pos += 1
                    continue

                # Check for IN keyword
                if self.text.startswith('in', self.pos):
                    # Make sure it's not part of an identifier
                    next_pos = self.pos + 2
                    if next_pos >= self.length or not self.text[next_pos].isalnum():
                        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IN, 'in', self.pos))
                        self.pos += 2
                        continue
input.new_string:
# Check for single char operators
                if char in self.SINGLE_CHAR_OPERATORS:
                    self.tokens.append(Token(self.SINGLE_CHAR_OPERATORS[char], char, self.pos))
                    self.pos += 1
                    continue

                # Check for keywords and identifiers
                start = self.pos
                while self.pos < self.length and (self.text[self.pos].isalnum() or self.text[self.pos] == '_'):
                    self.pos += 1
                ident = self.text[start:self.pos]

                # Check if it's a keyword
                if ident in self.KEYWORDS:
                    # Special handling for 'in' keyword
                    if ident == 'in':
                        # Make sure it's not part of a longer identifier
                        if self.pos == self.length or not self.text[self.pos].isalnum():
                            self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IN, 'in', start))
                            continue
                    elif ident in ('true', 'false'):
                        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.BOOL, ident, start))
                        continue
                    # For other keywords, we could map to specific token types
                    # but for simplicity, treat as IDENTIFIER (parser handles keywords)
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, ident, start))
                    continue
                else:
                    # It's an identifier
                    self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, ident, start))
                    continue
